claudeMd:
# Bug Fix: Blank Application

You are fixing a blank-screen bug in a Vite + TypeScript canvas game ("Quantum Runner") served via Docker + nginx.

## Root Cause

The app appears blank because:
1. The Dockerfile uses `nginx:alpine` with NO custom nginx config â€” the default config may not serve JS module files with correct MIME types, and lacks proper `try_files` handling
2. Vite builds with absolute paths (`/assets/...`) which break if there's any path mismatch in the serving layer
3. No `.dockerignore` exists, potentially causing build context issues

## What to Fix

### 1. Create `nginx.conf` (new file)

Create a minimal nginx configuration that properly serves a static SPA:

```nginx
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name _;
    root /usr/share/nginx/html;
    index index.html;

    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html;
    }

    location /assets/ {
        expires 1y;
        add_header Cache-Control "public, immutable";
    }

    gzip on;
    gzip_types text/plain text/css application/javascript application/json image/svg+xml;
    gzip_min_length 1000;
}
```

### 2. Update `Dockerfile`

Replace the nginx stage to use the custom config:

```dockerfile
FROM node:22-alpine AS build
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM nginx:alpine
COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
COPY --from=build /app/dist /usr/share/nginx/html
EXPOSE 80
```

Key change: `COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf` â€” this replaces the default server block with our custom one that properly handles static file serving.

### 3. Update `vite.config.ts`

Add `base: './'` so built assets use relative paths instead of absolute:

```typescript
import { defineConfig } from 'vite';

export default defineConfig({
  base: './',
  build: {
    target: 'ES2022',
  },
});
```

### 4. Create `.dockerignore` (new file)

```
node_modules
dist
.git
.github
*.md
```

### 5. Update `index.html` â€” add explicit canvas dimensions

Add width and height to the canvas element so it has visible dimensions even before JS loads:

```html
<canvas id="game-canvas" width="624" height="520"></canvas>
```

These values come from: `GRID_COLS * CELL_SIZE = 12 * 52 = 624` and `GRID_ROWS * CELL_SIZE = 10 * 52 = 520`.

## Conventions

- This is a vanilla TypeScript project with no framework
- Use ES2022 target
- Keep changes minimal â€” only fix the blank screen bug
- Do not add new dependencies
- Conventional Commits: use `fix(build):` prefix

## Verification

1. Run `npm run build` â€” must succeed
2. Run `npx vite preview` â€” app must serve and render title screen
3. Verify built HTML uses relative asset paths (`./assets/...` not `/assets/...`)
4. Verify `nginx.conf` is valid configuration

## Gotchas

- The canvas dimensions in `index.html` (624x520) must match `CONFIG.GRID_COLS * CONFIG.CELL_SIZE` and `CONFIG.GRID_ROWS * CONFIG.CELL_SIZE` from `src/config.ts`
- The nginx.conf goes to `/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf` (replacing the default server block), NOT `/etc/nginx/nginx.conf` (which is the main config)
- `base: './'` produces `./assets/...` paths in the built HTML â€” verify this in the output

checks: The game canvas is visible and renders a title screen with 'QUANTUM RUNNER' text in cyan/teal color on a dark background
Below the canvas, there are 'Accept [Enter]' and 'Pass [Space]' buttons (may be hidden initially on title screen)
The page background is very dark (#0a0a0f), not white or default browser gray
Clicking the canvas or pressing Enter starts the game, showing a grid-based game board with colored shapes
